body: This code needs to be cleaned up (constructors for objects should not return `nothing`) but in the interim, this may fix the issue on #4547
title: Add constructor for missing FieldBoundaryConditions

body:
This feature can build diagnostics that evaluate forcing functions. For example,

```julia
c_forcing_op = ForcingOperation(:c, model)
```

builds an `AbstractOperation` that evaluates the forcing on `c`. We also provide the shortcut

```julia
c_forcing_field = ForcingField(:c, model)
```
title: Implement ForcingOperation plus improvements to show

body:
I think this is the formulation we want because it is the absolute tolerance relative to the dt used.
In main, if dt is too small, multiplying the `ϵ` times dt might result in an absolute tolerance lower than machine precision.

I am not sure what tests were performed on #4505 to test that the new formulation worked but the previous did not, so I will try to produce some tests with different precisions and dt size to make sure this is the tolerance we want

closes #4549
title: Fix indexing issues for `FieldTimeSeries`

body:
Creates the function `bottommost_active_node` which can substitute some ad-hoc lines in the `ImmersedBoundaries` module, can eventually become the basis for a `MappedKernelOperation`, and can also be used in Oceanostics (see https://github.com/tomchor/Oceanostics.jl/pull/203).

cc @glwagner
title: Creates `bottommost_active_node` function
